children.heading: Establishment of Chaplain Corps
children.label: a Establishment of Chaplain Corps
children.text: There is in the Agency a Chaplain Corps, which shall provide spiritual and religious pastoral services and care across all components of the Agency for employees of all faiths and non-faiths.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The head of the Chaplain Corps shall be the Chief of Chaplains, who shall be appointed by the Director.

children.heading: Reporting
children.label: 2 Reporting
children.text: The Chief of Chaplains shall report directly to the Director.

children.label: A
children.text: oversee the Chaplain Corps; and

children.label: i
children.text: all guidance pertaining to chaplains’ care;

children.label: ii
children.text: programming and instruction; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any policy or guidance pertaining to religion or religious accommodation.

children.heading: Consultation
children.label: 4 Consultation
children.text: All appropriate offices of the Agency shall consult with the Chief of Chaplains on best practices to implement guidance or policy pertaining to religion or religious accommodation.
be located at the headquarters building of the Agency; and
travel as necessary to provide services to personnel of the Agency.

children.label: A
children.text: not less than 3 shall be full-time staff employees of the Agency; and

children.label: B
children.text: not less than 3 shall be government contractors.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: 2 Vacancies
children.text: The Director shall expeditiously fill any vacancies.

children.heading: Exclusive role
children.label: 3 Exclusive role
children.text: A chaplain of the Chaplain Corps shall serve exclusively in the chaplain’s role in the Chaplain Corps.

children.heading: Appointment; compensation
children.label: 4 Appointment; compensation
children.text: The Director may appoint and fix the compensation of such chaplains of the Chaplain Corps as the Director considers appropriate, except that the Director may not provide basic pay to any chaplain of the Chaplain Corps at an annual rate of basic pay in excess of the maximum rate of basic pay for grade GS–15 of the General Schedule under .section 5332 of title 5
provide security clearances, including one-time read-ins, to chaplains of the Chaplain Corps to ensure that personnel of the Agency can seek unrestricted chaplaincy counseling; and
furnish physical workspace for the Chaplain Corps at the headquarters building of the Agency.

children.heading: Privacy
children.label: f Privacy
children.text: The Director shall implement privacy standards with respect to the physical workspaces of the Chaplain Corps to ensure privacy for individuals visiting such spaces.

children.heading: Protection of Chaplain Corps
children.label: g Protection of Chaplain Corps
children.text: The Director may not require a chaplain of the Chaplain Corps to perform any rite, ritual, or ceremony that is contrary to the conscience, moral principles, or religious beliefs of the chaplain or of the ecclesiastical organization that ordains the chaplain.

children.heading: Certifications to Congress
children.label: h Certifications to Congress
children.text: Not less frequently than annually, the Director shall certify to Congress whether implementation of this section meets the requirements of this section.
